（'''Phoenix Hall'''）{{ill|Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|ja|北陸三十三ヵ所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|1=Hokuriku Kannon Pilgrimage Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404440}} 特番 |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.kurikara.or.jp/index.html |公式HP名 = Kurikara Fudō-ji |地図2 = }} [[File:kurikarafudouji hououden.jpg|thumb|250px|Phoenix Hall]] '''Kurikara Fudō-ji''' (倶利迦羅不動寺) is a temple of {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}, designated as a Bekkaku Honzan, located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|en|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130942|lt=Kahoku District|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, and in the area known as {{ill|Kurikara|ja|倶利伽羅 (津幡町)|lt=Kurikara|lt_ja=倶利伽羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Although some sources record the name as “倶利'''伽'''羅不動寺” in accordance with local usage, the official website consistently uses “倶利'''迦'''羅不動寺”. ==概要== {{Expand section}} '''Kurikara''' is derived from the Sanskrit {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} word “kulihah”. It means “the black dragon of complete fortune and virtue” and comes from the name of the principal image, in which a sword is entwined by a {{ill|Black Dragon|ja|黒龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒竜|1=black dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11288580}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kurikara.or.jp/history/index.html|title=About Kurikara Fudō-ji|publisher=Kurikara Fudō-ji|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{Cite news|title=Celebration Ceremony for the 1300th Foundation Year|newspaper=Hokkoku Shimbun|date=2018-04-29|page=28}} ==歴史== * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (718), it is said that Kurikara Fudō Myōō was enshrined by the imperial command of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Genshō Tennō|1=Empress Genshō}}. * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} (812), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) carved a Fudō-son statue identical to the principal image and established Chōraku-ji as a head temple. * In the 24th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1949), temple buildings were reconstructed on the site of the Chōraku-ji ruins, and the temple was restored as Kurikara Fudō-ji. [[File:Oman Jizo (Kurikara).jpg|thumb|人柱となった地元の少女を祀る「おまん地蔵」]] == Cultural Properties == === 絵画 === * Arhat statue: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} === 古文書 === * Letters of Minamoto no Yoritomo: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} （'''Phoenix Hall'''）{{ill|Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|ja|北陸三十三ヵ所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|1=Hokuriku Kannon Pilgrimage Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404440}} 特番 |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.kurikara.or.jp/index.html |公式HP名 = Kurikara Fudō-ji |地図2 = }} [[File:kurikarafudouji hououden.jpg|thumb|250px|Phoenix Hall]] '''Kurikara Fudō-ji''' (倶利迦羅不動寺) is a temple of {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}, designated as a Bekkaku Honzan, located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|en|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130942|lt=Kahoku District|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, and in the area known as {{ill|Kurikara|ja|倶利伽羅 (津幡町)|lt=Kurikara|lt_ja=倶利伽羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Although some sources record the name as “倶利'''伽'''羅不動寺” in accordance with local usage, the official website consistently uses “倶利'''迦'''羅不動寺”. ==概要== {{Expand section}} '''Kurikara''' is derived from the Sanskrit {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} word “kulihah”. It means “the black dragon of complete fortune and virtue” and comes from the name of the principal image, in which a sword is entwined by a {{ill|Black Dragon|ja|黒龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒竜|1=black dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11288580}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kurikara.or.jp/history/index.html|title=About Kurikara Fudō-ji|publisher=Kurikara Fudō-ji|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{Cite news|title=Celebration Ceremony for the 1300th Foundation Year|newspaper=Hokkoku Shimbun|date=2018-04-29|page=28}} ==歴史== * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (718), it is said that Kurikara Fudō Myōō was enshrined by the imperial command of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Genshō Tennō|1=Empress Genshō}}. * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} (812), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) carved a Fudō-son statue identical to the principal image and established Chōraku-ji as a head temple. * In the 24th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1949), temple buildings were reconstructed on the site of the Chōraku-ji ruins, and the temple was restored as Kurikara Fudō-ji. [[File:Oman Jizo (Kurikara).jpg|thumb|人柱となった地元の少女を祀る「おまん地蔵」]] == Cultural Properties == === 絵画 === * Arhat statue: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} * Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva statue: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} === 古文書 === * Letters of Minamoto no Yoritomo: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} === 史跡 === * Nanono Tomb Group (Graves 2, 3, 4): Historic Site of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|WD=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} == アクセス== * Mountain Top Main Hall ** 20 minutes by car from {{ill|IR Ishikawa Railway|en|IR Ishikawa Railway|WD=Q11585936|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} {{ill|Tsubata Station|en|Tsubata Station|WD=Q3701490|lt=Tsubata （'''Phoenix Hall'''）{{ill|Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|ja|北陸三十三ヵ所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|1=Hokuriku Kannon Pilgrimage Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404440}} 特番 |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.kurikara.or.jp/index.html |公式HP名 = Kurikara Fudō-ji |地図2 = }} [[File:kurikarafudouji hououden.jpg|thumb|250px|Phoenix Hall]] '''Kurikara Fudō-ji''' (倶利迦羅不動寺) is a temple of {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}, designated as a Bekkaku Honzan, located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|en|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|qid=Q1130942|lt=Kahoku District|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, and in the area known as {{ill|Kurikara|ja|倶利伽羅 (津幡町)|lt=Kurikara|lt_ja=倶利伽羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Although some sources record the name as “倶利'''伽'''羅不動寺” in accordance with local usage, the official website consistently uses “倶利'''迦'''羅不動寺”. ==概要== {{Expand section}} '''Kurikara''' is derived from the Sanskrit {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|qid=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} word “kulihah”. It means “the black dragon of complete fortune and virtue” and comes from the name of the principal image, in which a sword is entwined by a {{ill|Black Dragon|ja|黒龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒竜|1=black dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11288580}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kurikara.or.jp/history/index.html|title=About Kurikara Fudō-ji|publisher=Kurikara Fudō-ji|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{Cite news|title=Celebration Ceremony for the 1300th Foundation Year|newspaper=Hokkoku Shimbun|date=2018-04-29|page=28}} ==歴史== * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (718), it is said that Kurikara Fudō Myōō was enshrined by the imperial command of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Genshō Tennō|1=Empress Genshō}}. * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} (812), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) carved a Fudō-son statue identical to the principal image and established Chōraku-ji as a head temple. * In the 24th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1949), temple buildings were reconstructed on the site of the Chōraku-ji ruins, and the temple was restored as Kurikara Fudō-ji. [[File:Oman Jizo (Kurikara).jpg|thumb|人柱となった地元の少女を祀る「おまん地蔵」]] == Cultural Properties == === 絵画 === * Arhat statue: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} * Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva statue: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} === 古文書 === * Letters of Minamoto no Yoritomo: Designated Cultural Property of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} === 史跡 === * Nanono Tomb Group (Graves 2, 3, 4): Historic Site of {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} == アクセス== * Mountain Top Main Hall ** （'''Mountain Top Main Hall'''）{{ill|Hokuriku Hakujyu Kannon Reijō|ja|北陸白寿観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuriku Hakujyu Kannon Reijō|1=Q11404430|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404430}} 第18番
（'''Phoenix Hall'''）{{ill|Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|ja|北陸三十三ヵ所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuriku 33 Kannon Reijō|1=Hokuriku Kannon Pilgrimage Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404440}} 特番 |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.kurikara.or.jp/index.html |公式HP名 = Kurikara Fudō-ji |地図2 = }} [[File:kurikarafudouji hououden.jpg|thumb|250px|Phoenix Hall]] '''Kurikara Fudō-ji''' (倶利迦羅不動寺) is a temple of {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}, designated as a Bekkaku Honzan, located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|en|Kahoku District, Ishikawa|qid=Q1130942|lt=Kahoku District|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Tsubata, Ishikawa|en|Tsubata, Ishikawa|qid=Q432014|lt=Tsubata Town|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, and in the area known as {{ill|Kurikara|ja|倶利伽羅 (津幡町)|lt=Kurikara|lt_ja=倶利伽羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Although some sources record the name as “倶利'''伽'''羅不動寺” in accordance with local usage, the official website consistently uses “倶利'''迦'''羅不動寺”. ==概要== {{Expand section}} '''Kurikara''' is derived from the Sanskrit {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|qid=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} word “kulihah”. It means “the black dragon of complete fortune and virtue” and comes from the name of the principal image, in which a sword is entwined by a {{ill|Black Dragon|ja|黒龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒竜|1=black dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11288580}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kurikara.or.jp/history/index.html|title=About Kurikara Fudō-ji|publisher=Kurikara Fudō-ji|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{Cite news|title=Celebration Ceremony for the 1300th Foundation Year|newspaper=Hokkoku Shimbun|date=2018-04-29|page=28}} ==歴史== * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (718), it is said that Kurikara Fudō Myōō was enshrined by the imperial command of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Genshō Tennō|1=Empress Genshō}}. * In the 2nd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} (812), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) carved a Fudō-son statue identical to the principal image and established Chōraku-ji as a head temple. * In the 24th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1949), temple buildings were reconstructed on the site of the Chōraku-ji ruins, and the temple was restored as Kurikara Fudō-ji.
